skill.name: rag-pipeline
skill.description: Explains a Retrieval Augmented Generation pipeline for document ingestion, embeddings, Qdrant vector search, prompt construction, personalization, and agent flow.
skill.summary: Documents the ingestion, vector search, and prompt flow for a RAG system.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby/shell backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code spans in SKILL.md, and the weak cryptography blocker at SKILL.md:3 is not supported by the file contents. The skill is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it documents running an ingestion script that scans local docs, uses an embedding model, and upserts to Qdrant.

medium_findings.title: Documented Ingestion Can Send Local Content to External Services
medium_findings.description: The skill describes scanning docs, creating embeddings with models/text-embedding-004, and upserting vectors to Qdrant. This is legitimate RAG behavior, but users should avoid indexing confidential content without confirming model and vector database handling policies.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly documents local document scanning and embedding/vector database operations. Confidence is moderate because only documentation is present, not the implementation of backend/ingest.py.

medium_findings.title: User-Invoked Ingestion Command Requires Review Before Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run python backend/ingest.py. This appears to be normal project workflow guidance, but community skills that direct command execution should be treated carefully until the referenced script is reviewed in the consuming project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is clear evidence of a command users may run, but no evidence that the skill itself executes it or injects user input. The risk depends on the referenced script outside this skill file.

low_findings.title: Markdown Inline Code Misclassified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown inline code spans such as paths, package names, collection names, and one documented run command as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These are not executable Ruby constructs in this Markdown skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation, not Ruby code. The backticks are formatting delimiters around file paths, model names, package names, and identifiers.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Not Supported by Evidence
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography at SKILL.md:3, but that line is the skill description metadata. No cryptographic algorithm or hash function is present on the cited line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only a natural-language description of a RAG pipeline. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic usage in the cited file.

content.user_title: Build RAG Ingestion and Vector Search
content.value_statement: RAG projects need clear guidance for ingestion, embeddings, retrieval, and prompt assembly. This skill documents the pipeline components and execution flow for a Qdrant-backed RAG system.
actual_capabilities: Describes document ingestion from a docs directory.
Explains MDX cleaning, text chunking, and overlap settings.
Identifies Qdrant collection, vector size, and cosine similarity settings.
Explains how retrieved context is added to RAG prompts.
Notes personalization inputs for system instruction generation.
limitations: Does not include the ingestion script implementation.
Does not validate Qdrant configuration or credentials.
Does not provide deployment or monitoring instructions.
Does not define data retention or privacy controls.

use_cases.title: Map an Existing RAG Pipeline
use_cases.description: Review how ingestion, embeddings, vector storage, retrieval, and prompt construction fit together in one project.

use_cases.title: Onboard Contributors to Retrieval Code
use_cases.description: Give new contributors a concise reference for the files and concepts used by the RAG workflow.

use_cases.title: Prepare Documentation for RAG Operations
use_cases.description: Turn pipeline notes into operational documentation for ingestion runs and vector search behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-pipeline skill to explain the ingestion, embedding, Qdrant, and prompt engineering flow in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Onboarding Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-pipeline skill to draft an onboarding note for a developer who needs to understand the RAG files and data flow.

prompt_templates.title: Review Operational Risks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-pipeline skill to list the operational checks before running ingestion, including document scope and vector database settings.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Retrieval Improvement Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rag-pipeline skill to propose improvements for chunking, retrieval quality, prompt context assembly, and future agent workflows.

output_examples.input: Explain how documents become searchable context.
output_examples.output: Documents are scanned from the docs directory, cleaned, split into overlapping chunks, embedded, and stored in Qdrant for cosine similarity search.
The personalization component builds system instructions from the user software and hardware background before the final RAG prompt is sent.

output_examples.input: What should I check before ingestion?
output_examples.output: Confirm the document set is approved for indexing, verify embedding access, confirm the Qdrant collection settings, and review the ingestion script.

best_practices: Review source documents for sensitive data before ingestion.
Confirm embedding and vector database settings before each run.
Document chunking changes and retrieval quality observations.
anti_patterns: Indexing private documents without data handling approval.
Changing vector dimensions without rebuilding the collection.
Treating retrieved context as verified truth without source review.

faq.question: What does this skill document?
faq.answer: It documents a RAG pipeline with ingestion, embeddings, Qdrant vector search, prompt construction, personalization, and agent flow.

faq.question: Does this skill include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill contains Markdown documentation and references project files and one ingestion command.

faq.question: Which vector database is described?
faq.answer: The skill describes Qdrant and a collection named physical_ai_book.

faq.question: Which embedding model is mentioned?
faq.answer: The skill mentions models/text-embedding-004 with a 768-dimensional vector size.

faq.question: Can it help improve retrieval quality?
faq.answer: It can guide review of chunking, retrieval settings, and prompt context assembly, but it does not measure quality itself.

faq.question: What should users check before running ingestion?
faq.answer: Users should review the referenced ingestion script, document scope, embedding service handling, and Qdrant configuration.
